No Poverty.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to poverty eradication.

Zero Hunger.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to hunger eradication or food security.

Good Health and Well-being.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to health or well-being outcomes.

Quality Education.reason: As a university course, it directly provides quality tertiary education (Target 4.3) and develops 'independent research ability' and 'professional knowledge', relevant skills for employment (Target 4.4).
evidence: To provide students with knowledge of qualitative research method

Gender Equality.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to gender equality or women's empowerment.

Clean Water and Sanitation.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to water, sanitation, or hygiene.

Affordable and Clean Energy.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to energy access or renewable energy.

Decent Work and Economic Growth.reason: The course develops 'independent research ability' and 'academic publication ability,' which are high-value skills promoting productive activities and innovation (Target 8.3).

Industry, Innovation and Infrastructure.reason: The course's core methodology, grounded theory, focuses on 'construction of hypotheses and theories' and 'discover new theories,' directly enhancing scientific research and innovation (Target 9.5).

Reduced Inequalities.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus specifically addresses reducing inequalities.

Sustainable Cities and Communities.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to sustainable urbanization or community development.

Responsible Consumption and Production.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to sustainable consumption or production patterns.

Climate Action.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to climate change mitigation or adaptation.

Life Below Water.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to marine ecosystems or ocean health.

Life on Land.reason: No direct or indirect evidence in the course syllabus links to terrestrial ecosystems or biodiversity.

Peace, Justice and Strong Institutions.reason: The course includes 'Research Ethics,' which contributes to developing accountable and transparent institutions by fostering integrity in research practices (Target 16.6).

Partnerships for the Goals.reason: The course is part of an 'International Master's Program' and develops 'international participation ability,' enhancing international cooperation and capacity-building (Targets 17.6, 17.9).
evidence: International Master's Program of Applied Economics and Social Development
